channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Audio Sentiment Analysis via Distillation and Cross-Modal Integration of Generated Multilingual Transcripts
item.description: arXiv:2607.06611v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automatically recognizing the sentiment, positive or negative, from speech is a challenging task, requiring both the analysis of vocal inflections and the interpretation of uttered words. Recent solutions rely on audio foundation models to solve the t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, study, multimodal, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Gradient-Based Speech-to-Text Alignment for Any ASR Model: From CTC to Speech LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.06831v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speech-to-text alignment means finding the temporal boundaries of each word in the audio. Some models provide such an alignment directly and others do not. Connectionist temporal classification (CTC) and transducer models have an alignment by construc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, arxiv, study, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Comprehensive Evaluation of Large Language Model Responses: A Multi-Factor Scoring System
item.description: arXiv:2607.06940v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The remarkable performance of large language models (LLMs) in linguistic tasks underscores an urgent need for comprehensive evaluation of their response quality. Prevailing methods, often confined to singular dimensions, fall short of capturing the fu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, reasoning, framework, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Does In-Context Search Help? A Sampling-Complexity Theory of Reflection-Driven Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2607.06720v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) with extended reasoning has enabled in-context search, in which models iteratively generate, critique, and revise solution attempts. We provide a theoretical analysis of in-context search by modeling it as approxi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, study, in-context, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM-powered reasoning in agent-based modeling
item.description: arXiv:2607.06757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent-based modeling (ABM) has the capability to model millions of individuals and their interactions, which is useful for policy making. However, ABMs have traditionally relied on static prior, which prevents the models from adapting to real-time cha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, reasoning, framework, model, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cost-Effective Agent Harnesses for Abstract Reasoning and Generalization on ARC-AGI-1
item.description: arXiv:2607.06764v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent progress on ARC-AGI-1 from disclosed architectures has come broadly from two regimes: heavy test-time compute over frontier models (evolutionary search, exhaustive sampling, extended chain-of-thought), or benchmark-specific training in which sm...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, analysis, fine-tuning, study, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics
item.description: arXiv:2607.06820v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in AI for Mathematics have focused largely on autoformalization and theorem proving, leaving the role of Computer Algebra Systems (CAS) in agentic LLM workflows underexplored. We propose a ReAct-style agentic setup that combines LLM re...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, context, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: D2PO: Optimizing Diffusion Samplers via Dynamic Preference
item.description: arXiv:2607.06609v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose D2PO (Dynamic Direct Preference Optimization), a principled framework for optimizing diffusion sampling policies with respect to timestep schedules and classifier-free guidance (CFG) weights. Our work is motivated by a fundamental limitatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, RAG, framework, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: STAGformer: A Spatio-temporal Agent Graph Transformer for Micro Mobility Demand Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2607.06614v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate station-level demand forecasting is essential for the efficient operation of bike-sharing systems, yet it remains challenging due to complex spatio-temporal dependencies and the large scale of urban networks. This paper presents STAGformer, a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, transformer, attention, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NEST: Tackling Dataset-Level Distribution Shifts via Regime-Oriented Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: arXiv:2607.06607v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate long-term forecasting in complex systems is frequently compromised by dataset-level distribution shifts, where diverse underlying behavioral modes and evolving system states drive the dynamic multivariate time-series. While existing methods p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, attention, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QANTIS: Hardware-Calibrated Sequential POMDP Belief Updates on IBM Heron
item.description: arXiv:2607.06760v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous systems under partial observability act on beliefs, not raw sensor events. QANTIS treats the quantum processor as a calibrated belief-update service in that loop: it receives a prior and an observation model, estimates the rare-event eviden...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Reliability Based Bi-Objective Portfolio Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.06610v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Portfolio optimization under uncertainty is inherently a multi-objective decision problem involving complex interactions among return, risk, market dynamics, and practical investment constraints. Existing reliability based portfolio optimization appro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Inertia-1: An Open Exploration of Wearable Motion Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.06617v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wearable motion sensing provides a continuous and scalable window into human behavior and health, making it a natural fit for foundation models, yet its pretraining and scaling principles remain poorly understood. Prior work studies isolated design ch...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
